keeper: We do not yet know the torus. We know where its chamber balances.
admitted_claims: The affine centroid is a canonical translation-normalized anchor for the finite chamber.
Every chamber state may be represented relative to the affine centroid.
Transition differences are invariant under translation of the full chamber.
Source and daughter states may be compared in the centered affine frame.
The centroid may serve as the provisional anchor for later toroidal closure tests.
not_admitted: A geometry, physics, force, or Planck-scale claim.

proof_obligation_retune.PO5_seed_independence: retained from prior binary closure audit

next_derivation_frame: candidate toroidal winding only if independently derived
boundary: The centroid is admitted as the only current anchor.
The centroid does not establish a torus embedding.
Centered transition vectors are translation invariant.
No axis, winding, metric, carrier law, theorem, physics, force, or Gap A closure claim.
